headlines.title: How the Iran war has left Europe facing yet another energy crisis
headlines.description: It is not the first time that there has been deep energy-linked frustration in the heart of Europe.

headlines.title: All doctors in England warned to watch for meningitis symptoms after Kent outbreak
headlines.description: Health workers across England are urged to look out for signs of infection as thousands have jabs.

headlines.title: Pay grows at slowest rate in more than five years
headlines.description: Annual earnings grew at an annual rate of 3.8% in the November to January period, the Office for National Statistics says.

headlines.title: World's longest coastal path opens in England
headlines.description: The King Charles coastal path will allow walkers right of access to the entire coast for the first time.

headlines.title: 'Designer' dog owners report more problem behaviours, vets warn
headlines.description: The Royal Veterinary College says popular "doodle" dogs do not always behave as expected.

headlines.title: Better NHS care might have saved 58 babies, BBC finds
headlines.description: There are growing calls for a statutory public inquiry into maternity services in Oxford.

headlines.title: Child seen in sex abuse videos identified after researcher spots school badge
headlines.description: An analyst tells the BBC how she tracked down a victim of child sexual abuse after years of searching.

headlines.title: Two men charged with allegedly spying on London Jewish community for Iran
headlines.description: Counter Terrorism Policing had been investigating alleged surveillance of locations and individuals linked to London's Jewish community.

headlines.title: Warm settled weather set to continue into the weekend
headlines.description: The spring-like weather is set to continue for the rest of the week but is unlikely to hit the highs of Wednesday's warmest day of the year so far, as Simon King explains.

headlines.title: US aircraft carrier to sail to Crete for repairs after fire on board
headlines.description: The USS Gerald R Ford has played a significant role in US operations during the war with Iran.

headlines.title: Interest rates expected to be held due to Iran war
headlines.description: Before the conflict began, analysts had expected a cut in the Bank rate at this meeting.

headlines.title: Why has this meningitis outbreak spread so fast?
headlines.description: There have been 20 cases since the weekend in one small area of Kent - but this isn't the normal pattern, so what could have happened?

headlines.title: This is the first time I've left my room since the outbreak
headlines.description: Twenty people are confirmed or suspected to have fallen ill with meningitis in the Canterbury area.

headlines.title: Watch: Why 'unprecedented' outbreak isn't another Covid
headlines.description: Meningitis infections result from close and prolonged physical contact and spreads more slowly than Covid.

headlines.title: Why is MenB vaccine not given to teenagers in UK and should they be offered it?
headlines.description: Students and older teens have not been vaccinated against the meningitis strain behind the Kent outbreak.

headlines.title: Ryan Gosling on bringing humour to sci-fi adventure Project Hail Mary
headlines.description: The Canadian actor stars as a science teacher turned astronaut in sci-fi film Project Hail Mary.

headlines.title: Chris Mason: Is government going wobbly on its migration plans?
headlines.description: Chris Mason asks if the UK government will stick to its big immigration shake-up or water it down.

headlines.title: The Papers: Labour migrant plans under scrutiny and Iran gas field targeted
headlines.description: A number of papers lead on whether the UK government is stalling on its migrant reform plans.

headlines.title: Alberta seeks to set limits on use of medically assisted dying
headlines.description: The Canadian province has proposed legislation that will limit its use solely to end-of-life circumstances.

headlines.title: South Africans say criminal gangs are exploiting the water crisis
headlines.description: Residents of some areas have been without mains water for weeks.

headlines.title: 'One of a kind' Messi hits 900 career goals - numbers behind milestone
headlines.description: After Lionel Messi scored his 900th career goal on Wednesday, BBC Sport looks at the numbers behind the "insane" achievement.

headlines.title: Afghan evacuees in limbo in Qatar camp accuse US of betrayal
headlines.description: More than 1,100 Afghans evacuated by the US fear their route to promised resettlement is closed for good.

headlines.title: The Iran war is causing a global energy crisis - can China withstand it?
headlines.description: With oil supply disrupted, Beijing's oil reserves and renewable energy push are being put to the test.

headlines.title: Oil crosses $110 a barrel after gas field strike
headlines.description: Iran's military warned it would take "decisive action" in response to the strike on its energy infrastructure.

headlines.title: Israel destroys river bridges in southern Lebanon
headlines.description: The health ministry says that 968 people, including more than 100 children, have been killed since 2 March.

headlines.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
headlines.description: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

headlines.title: Labubu film is official with Paddington director at the helm
headlines.description: The film will combine live action and computer-generated animation and is in "early development".

headlines.title: King praises 'living bridge' with Nigeria at glitzy banquet
headlines.description: At the state banquet in Windsor Castle, King Charles praises the UK's partnership with Nigeria.

headlines.title: UK sets target to boost steel making and cut imports
headlines.description: Up to half of steel used in Britain should be made there, the government says, as it announces its steel strategy.

headlines.title: Amazon's Alexa has had an AI upgrade. Now she's got more to say
headlines.description: There has been criticism the Echo has stagnated, while other AI chatbots have become much easier to communicate with.

headlines.title: Beeps, shouts and abuse - police join female runners to tackle harassment
headlines.description: More than half of female runners in the UK have experienced harassment or abuse, research shows.

headlines.title: U-turn after job applicant told 'car is too old'
headlines.description: Alanah Thompson French, 18, was originally told by haart her application could not be progressed.

headlines.title: Sadiq Khan says Labour should pledge to rejoin EU
headlines.description: Downing Street has repeatedly said the government will not rejoin the single market or customs union.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: (Another) Labour Leadership Challenge?
headlines.description: Angela Rayner sparks leadership speculation claiming Labour is “running out of time”.

headlines.title: Trump threatens TV networks over unpatriotic Iran coverage
headlines.description: And why has top counterterrorism official Joe Kent resigned?

headlines.title: Four English teams out in 24 hours - what happened and why?
headlines.description: With just two out of six Premier League teams progressing to the Champions League quarter-finals, what went wrong for the English sides?

headlines.title: 'I thought I'd be bleeped by BBC' - but Hunt's words resonate
headlines.description: British sprinter Amy Hunt on inspiring female athletes to get university degrees as she prepares to compete at the World Athletics Indoor Championships.

headlines.title: Credit to Tudor - is he turning around Spurs side who looked lost?
headlines.description: Tottenham can take encouragement in their battle for Premier League survival despite a Champions League exit, writes Phil McNulty.

headlines.title: Slot needed a performance - and Liverpool delivered one
headlines.description: Despite a difficult domestic campaign, Liverpool produce one of their best displays of the season against Galatasaray in the Champions League.

headlines.title: Tudor gets first Tottenham win but Spurs lose tie against Atletico Madrid
headlines.description: Tottenham beat Atletico Madrid 3-2 in the second leg of their last 16 tie in the Champions League, securing a first win for manager Igor Tudor but it's not enough to win the tie, as Atletico win 7-5 on aggregate to progress to the quarter finals.

headlines.title: The Man Utd prodigy still too young to play senior football
headlines.description: At 15, Manchester United's JJ Gabriel is too young to play in the Premier League but he is already showing clear signs he is a huge talent.
